title: slow-cooked mexican beef stew
description: rich, chewy, and flavorful mexican stew featuring ground nutmeg, served with warm flour tortillas. perfect for a date-night dinner

ingredients.name: ground beef substitute (such as lentils or mushrooms)

instructions: heat oil in a large kazan-cauldron over medium-high heat. add the onion and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
add the garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
add the ground beef substitute, cumin, oregano, and nutmeg. cook, breaking up with a spoon, until the mixture is lightly browned, about 5 minutes.
add the chili powder and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
add the diced tomatoes and beef broth. bring to a simmer.
reduce heat to low and cook, covered, for 2 hours, until the stew is thick and the flavors have melded together.
season with salt and pepper to taste.
warm the flour tortillas by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
serve the stew hot, garnished with a dollop of sour cream and a sprinkle of shredded cheese.
offer warm flour tortillas on the side for wrapping.
reheat the stew, if needed, before serving
